abolisher.name: Abolisher
description: ## Abolisher
‹SOURCE Monster Hunter's Handbook/7›
Abolishers are incorruptible inquisitors who weed out creatures of alien, unnatural origins, finding and exposing aberrations for what they are.

::ab[Sworn to Purity (Ex)]{icon=warning choice="An abolisher must select one of the following domains: ‹domain/Air›, ‹domain/Animal›, ‹domain/Earth›, ‹domain/Fire›, ‹domain/Plant›, ‹domain/Water›, or ‹domain/Weather›. If she ever changes her deity to one that has none of the listed domains, she loses this archetype and becomes a normal inquisitor or ex-inquisitor." alter=Domains}

::ab[Revealing Gaze (Ex)]{icon=boost passive="An abolisher receives a morale bonus on opposed Perception checks against other creatures' Disguise and Stealth checks equal to ‹misc/half› her inquisitor level (minimum +1). She grants this bonus to all adjacent allies." replace="Stern gaze"}

::ab[Expose Aberration (Sp)]{l=2 icon=magic-power ability="An abolisher can use ‹spell/detect aberration› at will. When she hits a creature with a weapon attack, she automatically knows if the creature is an aberration or not." imp5="She can activate her *bane* ability as an @HLimmediate_action after she hits an aberration but before damage is rolled." replace="Detect alignment" alter=Bane}

::ab[Escape Corruption's Grasp (Sp)]{l=5 icon=power flavor="An abolisher is divinely protected against the grasping tentacles and paralyzing secretions of aberrations." immediate="She can ignore impediments to movement, as per ‹spell/freedom of movement›." useL=inquisitor useNC replace="Discern lies"}

cloaked_wolf.name: Cloaked Wolf
description: ## Cloaked Wolf
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/28›
Some inquisitors are adept at appearing harmless until they throw off their unassuming facades and take their enemies by surprise.
::cskill{gain=soh lose=intm}

::ab[Lure Prey (Ex)]{l=1 icon=boost flavor="Cloaked wolves are skilled at maintaining a harmless guise even while they silently prepare to strike against their enemies." passive="A cloaked wolf receives a morale bonus on all Disguise and Sleight of Hand checks equal to ‹misc/half› her inquisitor level (minimum +1). When a cloaked wolf uses Sleight of Hand to draw a hidden weapon, this bonus doubles." replace="Stern gaze"}

::ab[Always Wary (Ex)]{flavor="A cloaked wolf is always ready for betrayal and subterfuge." l1="Whenever she attempts a Sense Motive check to avoid being surprised by a foe using Bluff to conceal the attack, the cloaked wolf can always act during the surprise round, regardless of the result of the check." l2="She adds her Wisdom modifier (in addition to her Dexterity modifier) to her initiative checks during any such encounter." alter="Cunning initiative" icon=power-boost replace="Monster lore"}

:::ab{l=3 icon=power action="At 6th Level" ability="A cloaked wolf can transition between parley and combat in the blink of an eye. She gains ‹feat/Quick Draw› as a bonus feat." order=aC title="Unleashed Fury (Ex)" replace="Solo tactics~teamwork feats" incrementOrd=The~6~3 incrementDesc="inquisitor gains a~cloaked wolf chooses a" incrementEnd=" bonus feat from the list above." special="She must meet the prerequisites of any bonus feat she selects."}

She gains a bonus feat from the following list: ‹feat/Alertness›, ‹feat/Catch Off-Guard›, ‹feat/Deceitful›, ‹feat/Deft Hands›, ‹feat/Dodge›, ‹feat/Improved Initiative›, ‹feat/Improved Unarmed Strike›, ‹feat/Improvised Weapon Mastery›, ‹feat/Lookout›, ‹feat/Mobility›, ‹feat/Spring Attack›, ‹feat/Stunning Fist›, and ‹feat/Throw Anything›.

As a @HLstandard_action, a cloaked wolf can choose to learn a new bonus feat from the above list in place of the most recent bonus feat she has already learned. In effect, the cloaked wolf loses the bonus feat in exchange for the new one. She can only change the most recent bonus feat gained. Whenever she gains a new bonus feat, the previous bonus feat becomes set and cannot be changed again.
A cloaked wolf can change her most recent bonus feat a number of times per day equal to her Wisdom modifier (minimum 1).

cold_iron_warden.name: Cold Iron Warden
description: ## Cold Iron Warden
‹SOURCE Demon Hunter's Handbook/15›

The natural enemies of ‹pclass/demoniac«s› and other cultists of fiendish forces, cold iron wardens are inquisitors who dedicate their lives to eradicating the taint of ‹family/demon«kind› from Golarion.

::ab[Track Teleportation (Su)]{l=2 icon=power flavor="A cold iron warden may acquire clues about a teleporting creature's destination by closely examining the place it once stood." fullround="While standing in a square that a creature occupied when it cast a conjuration (teleportation) spell or spell-like ability, the cold iron warden may attempt a Survival check (DC 10 + the teleportation spell's caster level) to sense the direction of the creature's destination as well as whether the destination lies within 25 feet, 100 feet, 1,000 feet, or more than 1,000 feet of her. She must use this ability within a number of rounds after the creature's teleportation equal to 1/2 her inquisitor level, after which the trail goes cold." useInc="4~inquisitor~2" imp12="If the cold iron warden exceeds the Survival check DC by 5 or more, she can also sense the creature's location as if using the ‹spell/locate creature› spell, using her inquisitor level as the caster level." imp18="If the cold iron warden exceeds the Survival check DC by 10 or more, she can also sense the creature's location as if she had cast ‹spell/discern location›." replace="Track~teamwork feats gained at 12th and 18th level"}

::ab[Abyssal Scourge (Su)]{l=3 icon=power-lower order=cua choice="A cold iron warden gains ‹feat/Alignment Channel› as a bonus feat and must choose either chaotic or evil outsiders." useMod=Charisma3 ability="She can ‹ability/channel energy› as a cleric of her inquisitor level - 2, but only to use Alignment Channel to damage outsiders. The DC of the Will save to halve the damage is equal to 10 + 1/2 the inquisitor's level + the inquisitor's Charisma modifier." imp6="The energy lingers in the affected area for 1 round as a shimmering, barely perceptible, mist that disrupts Abyssal magic. A creature in the mist that casts a conjuration (summoning) spell or spell-like ability with the selected alignment subtype must succeed at a ‹rule/concentration› check (DC 15 + double the level of the conjuration spell) or lose the spell." imp15="Any chaotic or evil creature that casts a conjuration (teleportation) spell or spell-like ability while in the mist must succeed at a concentration check (same DC as above) or lose the spell." special="A cold iron warden can take other feats to add to this ability such as ‹feat/Improved Channel›, but not feats that alter this ability, such as ‹feat/Command Undead›." replace="Solo tactics~teamwork feats gained at 6th and 15th level"}

::ab[Favored Judgment]{icon=power l3="The inquisitor gains ‹feat/Favored Judgment› as a bonus feat but must choose either outsider (chaotic) or outsider (evil)." l9="She gains Favored Judgment as a bonus feat a second time and must choose either outsider (chaotic) or outsider (evil)." replace="Teamwork feats gained at 3rd and 9th level"}

::ab[Bane (Su)]{icon=down-boost passive="A cold iron warden reduces the amount of bonus damage dealt when using her bane ability against creatures that are not outsiders with the chaotic or evil subtype by 1d6. She increases the amount of bonus damage by 1d6 when she selects outsiders with the chaotic or evil subtype." alter=Bane}

::ab[Translate Telepathy (Su)]{l=5 icon=power order=ai ability="An inquisitor can sense when a creature within 5 feet of her receives or sends a telepathic message." immediate="By making a Perception check (DC 15 + the highest CR of the creatures using telepathy), she may listen to all telepathic messages sent to and by creatures in this area for a number of rounds per day equal to her inquisitor level. These rounds do not need to be consecutive." incrementPlain="The distance at which she can detect telepathic messages~5~5~10~5" incrementEnd=" feet." replace="Discern lies"}

exarch.name: Exarch
description: ## Exarch
‹SOURCE Advanced Race Guide/13›

The gruff traditionalism of most dwarves finds its apex in those who adhere to a strict orthodoxy rooted in ancient principles and practices and who are not amenable whatsoever to change or innovation.
::prereq{r=Dwarf}
::ab[Spells]{icon=down note="Exarchs cannot cast spells with the chaotic descriptor." alter=Spellcasting}

::ab[Inflexible Will (Ex)]{l=1 icon=def passive="An exarch gains a +2 bonus on saving throws against ‹spell/confusion› and ‹spell/insanity› effects and effects with the chaotic descriptor." replace="Monster lore"}
::ab[Detect Chaos (Sp)]{icon=power ability="At will, an exarch can use ‹spell/detect chaos›." replace="Detect alignment"}

::ab[Fearsome Jurist (Su)]{l=5 icon=boost swift="An exarch can imbue one of her weapons with the ‹magic-enh/jurist› or ‹magic-enh/menacing› weapon special ability, and may switch between these properties as another swift action. When using either special ability, her weapon's critical threat range doubles against chaotic creatures. This does not stack with ‹spell/keen edge›, ‹feat/Improved Critical›, or similar effects. This ability otherwise functions as *bane.*" replace=Bane}

::ab[Aura of Repetition (Su)]{l=8 icon=aura standard="Once per day while using her judgment, an exarch can project an *aura of repetition,* as the ‹domain/Toil› subdomain power." special="If the exarch takes Artifice (Toil) as her domain, the save DC of her aura increases by 2 but its duration does not increase." replace="Second judgment"}

::ab[Double Jeopardy (Su)]{l=12 icon=boost passive="Whenever an exarch uses her *fearsome jurist* ability, she may choose to affect two weapons, with one gaining the *jurist* weapon special ability and the other the *menacing* special ability as above. Both special abilities may be combined in a single weapon, whose critical threat range doubles. This does not stack with *keen edge,* Improved Critical, or similar effects." replace="Greater bane"}

::ab[Aura of Reversion (Su)]{l=16 icon=aura-lower ability="While using her judgment, an exarch can project a 30-foot-radius ‹misc/emanation› for a number of rounds per day equal to her inquisitor level. Any creature other than the exarch that is using a transmutation effect within this aura at the beginning of its turn becomes ‹misc/sickened›, or sickened and ‹misc/nauseated› if using a polymorph effect, including the ‹umr/change shape› ability (Fortitude negates; DC 10 + 1/2 the exarch's level + her Wisdom modifier). Continuous effects from permanent magical items do not cause this effect. Within the aura, ‹spell/dispel› checks against transmutation effects gain a +4 bonus. This ability cannot be used simultaneously with *aura of repetition.*" replace="Third judgment"}

exorcist.name: Exorcist
description: ## Exorcist
‹SOURCE Ultimate Magic/44›

Some inquisitors, as they learn more about the threat of possession and the machinations of the planes, task themselves to expel possessing spirits and conniving outsiders from the world whenever possible. Eventually they learn the secret of the *verdicts of exorcism, exile,* and *anathema.*

::ab[Verdict of Exorcism (Su)]{l=8 icon=power ability="An inquisitor using *judgment* can unleash the *verdict of exorcism* on a creature. When she does, her judgment ends, but the creature is ‹misc/dazed› for 1 round (Will negates); if the creature is possessed, the possessing entity must succeed at a Will saving throw (DC 10 + 1/2 inquisitor level + inquisitor's Wisdom bonus), or be exorcised and never again allowed in that same body." replace="Second judgment"}

::ab[Verdict of Exile (Su)]{l=16 icon=power ability="An inquisitor using *judgment* can unleash the *verdict of exile* on a creature. When she does, her judgment ends, but the creature subject to that judgment is dazed for 1 round (Will negates, DC of 10 + 1/2 inquisitor level + Wisdom bonus). If the creature is possessed or an outsider, the possessing entity or the outsider is affected by ‹spell/dismissal› (Will negates)." replace="Third judgment"}

::ab[Closed Mind (Su)]{icon=def l=17 flavor="An exorcist inquisitor must close off her mind to deny her enemies even a foothold." passive="The inquisitor becomes immune to compulsion effects and possession attempts (including ‹spell/magic jar›)." replace=Slayer}

::ab[Verdict of Anathema (Su)]{l=20 icon=boost ability="An inquisitor using *judgment* can unleash the *verdict of anathema* on her enemies. When she does, her judgment ends, and all enemy creatures within 10 feet are affected by her *verdict of exorcism.*" replace="True judgment"}

expulsionist.name: Expulsionist
description: ## Expulsionist
‹SOURCE Haunted Heroes Handbook/14›

Most common among ‹faith/Pharasm«ins<a› but found within any faith that loathes undead or evil spirits, expulsionists are inquisitors who seek to rid the material world of the corrupting influences of wicked spirits that twist mortals to their own ends.

::ab[Expel Spirit (Su)]{l=1 icon=power-aura-touch useMod=Charisma3 passive="An expulsionist receives ‹feat/Alignment Channel› and ‹feat/Turn Undead› as bonus feats." ability="She can ‹ability/channel energy› a number of times per day equal to 3 + her Charisma modifier as per a cleric with an effective level equal to her inquisitor level, but only to harm or turn evil outsiders or undead (treating all evil outsiders as undead creatures for the purpose of determining whether they can be affected by Turn Undead). The DC to save against this ability is equal to 10 + 1/2 the expulsionist's inquisitor level + her Charisma modifier." special="She can take other feats that add to this ability, such as ‹feat/Extra Channel› or ‹feat/Improved Channel›, but not other feats that fundamentally alter this ability, such as ‹feat/Elemental Channel›, and she cannot select Alignment Channel multiple times." order=pau imp8="An expulsionist can expend one use of her channel energy ability to attempt to force a possessing creature from its host body. The expulsionist must make a melee touch attack against the possessed creature; if successful, the expulsionist deals damage to the possessing creature as if it were targeted by the expulsionist's channel energy ability (regardless of the possessing creature's creature type), and the possession effect ceases as the possessing creature is ejected from the host body. A successful Will save (DC = the expulsionist's channel energy DC) halves the damage taken and negates the expulsion." replace=Domain}

::ab[Expulsionist Lore (Ex)]{l=1 icon=boost passive="An expulsionist gains a bonus equal to 1/2 her inquisitor level (minimum +1) on skill checks to notice ‹rule/haunts› and incorporeal creatures; on Knowledge (religion) checks to identify the abilities and weaknesses of haunts and incorporeal creatures; and on Sense Motive checks to determine whether a creature is possessed, under the effects of an enchantment or curse, or otherwise magically controlled." replace="Monster lore"}

::ab[Spirit Sleuth (Ex)]{l=5 icon=power free="Upon interacting with a rejuvenating undead (such as a ‹template/ghost›) or haunt for the first time (such as by attacking the ghost or channeling energy to damage the haunt), an expulsionist can attempt a Sense Motive check to determine the specific course of action required to permanently neutralize the spirit of haunt (DC = 20 + the target's CR)." replace="Discern lies"}

faith_hunter.name: Faith Hunter
description: ## Faith Hunter
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/28›
Certain relentless inquisitors burn with special hatred for an enemy of specific convictions, and they stop at nothing when obsessively hunting these foes down.

::ab[Sworn Enemy (Sp, Su)]{icon=power-boost choice="A faith hunter chooses a single alignment (such as chaotic evil, rather than simply chaotic or evil) that directly opposes her own along either the law/chaos axis or the good/evil axis (so a lawful good faith hunter can choose chaotic good, chaotic neutral, chaotic evil, neutral evil, or lawful evil, whereas a neutral good faith hunter can only choose chaotic evil, neutral evil, or lawful evil). Creatures of this alignment are her **sworn enemies.**" ability="She can detect creatures of the chosen alignment at will, functioning as ‹spell/detect evil›, except this ability works only on creatures and detects the specific alignment." order=ca replace="Domain~justice and destruction judgments" alter=Judgment imp6="Once per day as a @HLswift_action, a faith hunter can smite one foe she can see. If the foe is of the faith hunter's *sworn enemy* alignment, the faith hunter gains a +4 sacred bonus on attack rolls against that foe and a sacred bonus equal to half her inquisitor level on damage rolls made against the foe. Smites against targets of the faith hunter's *sworn enemy* alignment automatically bypass any DR the creature might possess. If the faith hunter targets a creature that is not of her *sworn enemy* alignment, the smite is wasted with no effect." special="The smite effect remains until the foe is dead or the next time the faith hunter rests and regains her uses of this ability. At 10th level and every 4 inquisitor levels thereafter, a faith hunter can smite her enemies one additional time per day, to a maximum of four times per day at 18th level. A faith hunter does not gain the justice or destruction judgments." incrementPlain="A faith hunter can now~6~4~2" incrementDesc=smite incrementEnd=" times a day." }

::ab[Enemy Revealed (Su)]{l=2 icon=lower ability="When a faith hunter uses her *sworn enemy* ability to identify a creature's alignment (typically on the third round of concentrating on an area including the creature), she can reveal that creature's alignment aura, causing it to glow for 1 round per inquisitor level so that even creatures with regular vision can see the aura. This not only allows others to recognize the creature's alignment, potentially exposing its true nature, it also allows them to track its location easily by the glow. Even if such a creature is invisible, the telltale glow pinpoints the creature's square or squares." useMod=Wisdom useM special="This doesn't take any extra action on her part; this ability is part of the same action as concentrating on her *sworn enemy* detect ability." replace="Detect alignment"}
::ab[Hateful Bane (Su)]{l=5 icon=down passive="A faith hunter's *bane* ability functions only against creatures that are both the selected creature type and her *sworn enemy* alignment." alter=Bane}

green_faith_marshal.name: Green Faith Marshal
description: ## Green Faith Marshal
‹SOURCE Ultimate Wilderness/141;Heroes of the Wild/24›

The ‹faith/Green Faith› marshal serves as a literal force of nature, hunting down and punishing those who have committed sins against nature through enslavement and mistreatment of animals or destruction of wild places.

::ab[Power of Nature]{icon=power-learn ability="A Green Faith marshal gains one domain selected from the ‹ability/druid>animal and terrain» domains›. The 1st- through 6th-level bonus spells from this domain are added to the Green Faith marshal's spell list." alter=Domain replace="Stern gaze"}
::ab[Wild Lore (Ex)]{icon=boost passive="A Green Faith marshal adds her Wisdom modifier to her Knowledge (nature) skill checks, in addition to her Intelligence modifier." replace="Monster lore"}
::ab[Nature's Ally (Sp)]{l=5 icon=magic ability="A Green Faith marshal gains the ability to cast ‹spell/commune with nature› once per week." replace="Discern lies"}

::ab[Wild Step (Ex)]{l=11 icon=power passive="A Green Faith marshal can move through any sort of naturally occurring ‹rule/difficult terrain› (such as natural thorns, briars, overgrown areas, and similar terrain) at her normal speed without taking damage or suffering any other impairment. Terrain that is enchanted or magically manipulated to impede motion still affects her." replace=Stalwart}

heretic.name: Heretic
description: ## Heretic
‹SOURCE Ultimate Magic/45›

While all inquisitors hunt the enemies of the faith, sometimes, either through political maneuvering by her enemies or an unyielding tenacity that breaks her faith's basic tenets, an inquisitor can find herself a heretic. Still unyielding in her cause, these heretics are accustomed to using guile and deception to hide themselves and their activities while they continue to hunt their enemies.
::ab[Judgment (Su)]{head icon=power ability="A heretic gains the following judgment in addition to the normal list of inquisitor judgments."}

::ab[Escape (Su)]{next icon=def ability="Each time the inquisitor using this judgment hits an opponent with a melee or ranged attack, she can use a @HLmove_action attempt to create a diversion to hide (see the ‹skill/Stealth› skill)."}

::ab[Lore of Escape (Ex)]{l=1 icon=boost flavor="The heretic uses every trick she knows to escape those now pursuing her." passive="She adds her Wisdom modifier on Bluff and Stealth skill checks in addition to the normal ability score modifiers." replace="Monster lore"}

::ab[Hide Tracks (Ex)]{l=1 icon=def flavor="A heretical inquisitor is adept at hiding her tracks." passive="Creatures attempting to track her take a -5 penalty on rolls to find or follow her tracks."}
### Ex-Inquisitors

An inquisitor who becomes an ex-inquisitor can, with the GM's permission, take the heretic archetype, replacing her class abilities with the appropriate archetype abilities. If the character atones or joins a different faith, she loses her heretic abilities and regains her previous inquisitor class abilities.

hexenhammer.name: Hexenhammer
description: ## Hexenhammer
‹SOURCE Horror Adventures/52›

Some inquisitors embrace the dark powers of their traditional foes, turning such powers toward their own ends in order to pronounce judgment on those who oppose them. These inquisitors learn to use the hexes and some spells of witches, but must pay the price for dealing with supernatural powers, as they slip ever closer to the evil they seek to destroy.

::ab[Withering Gaze (Su)]{l=1 icon=magic flavor="Hexenhammers study the effects of a witch's evil eye, and can use this dark magic to steel their gaze in a similar manner." ability="Whenever a hexenhammer successfully demoralizes a creature as a standard action, instead of applying the ‹misc/shaken› condition, she can apply the effects of the witch ‹hex/evil eye› hex as if she were a 1st-level witch (save DC = 10 + 1/2 the hexenhammer's inquisitor level + her Wisdom modifier)." replace="Monster lore~stern gaze"}

::ab[Pride and Penance (Ex)]{icon=down flavor="Hexenhammers are deeply conflicted about their dealings with dark powers." note="If a hexenhammer uses a hex or casts a spell gained from the ‹main/witch« class» spell« list›, she temporarily loses access to her domain abilities and her *cunning mind* bonus until she undergoes a brief period of penance and atonement that takes 1 minute of quiet prayer and contemplation; this atonement doesn't require any additional cost, and it automatically restores the lost abilities, regardless of how many times the hexenhammer uses her dark powers. If the hexenhammer uses her *dark trade* ability (see below) to trade uses of judgment for hexes, she loses access to her domain abilities and *cunning mind* bonus until the next time she regains her spell slots for the day." alter=Domain}

::ab[Cunning Mind (Ex)]{l=2 icon=def passive="A hexenhammer gains a +2 bonus on saving throws against hexes, curses, spells of the necromancy school, and spells with the evil descriptor." replace="Cunning initiative"}

::ab[Hexcrafter (Su)]{l=3 icon=magic flavor="A hexenhammer learns to turn some powers of witches to her own advantage." ability="She learns either the ‹hex/blight›, the ‹hex/misfortune›, or the ‹hex/slumber› hex from the witch's hex list, and can use that hex once per day as a @HLstandard_action, using her inquisitor level in place of a witch level. If the hex allows a saving throw, the save DC to resist the hex is equal to 10 + 1/2 the inquisitor's level + the inquisitor's Wisdom modifier." incrementAt="The hexenhammer~6~12~15~2/1" incrementDesc="learns a~gains a" incrementOrd incrementEnd=" hex from her list of available hexes." imp9="The hexenhammer can use any one of her hexes one additional time per day." imp18="She can use any one of her hexes an additional time per day." imp12="The hexenhammer adds the ‹hex/agony›, ‹hex/nightmares›, and ‹hex/retribution› major hexes to that list." replace="Teamwork feats"}

::ab[Dark Trade (Su)]{l=3 icon=magic flavor="A hexenhammer can forgo the power of her judgments and succumb to the temptation of darker powers." ability="She can trade a daily use of judgment to instead gain an additional use of one of her hexes." replace="Solo tactics"}

::ab[Witchcraft (Ex)]{l=5 icon=learn ability="And at every 3 inquisitor levels thereafter, a hexenhammer can learn a witch spell in place of a spell she already knows. In effect, she loses an old spell in exchange for a new one selected from the witch class spell list. The new spell's level must be the same as that of the spell being exchanged, and it must be at least 1 level lower than the highest-level inquisitor spell she can cast." incrementOrd="She can learn~5~3" incrementDesc=a incrementEnd=" spell, as above." special="The hexenhammer can swap out only a single spell at any given level and must choose whether or not to swap the spell at the same time she gains new spells known for the level." alter=Spellcasting}

iconoclast.name: Iconoclast
description: ## Iconoclast
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/52›
Some magic items are heretical by nature, enabling the unfaithful to spread wickedness. Iconoclasts seek out and remove such crutches, cleansing the taint these items exude.
::ab[Shake Effects (Ex)]{l=1 icon=def passive="An iconoclast gains a +2 bonus on all saving throws against effects that come from a magic item." replace="Monster lore"}
::ab[Detect Magic (Sp)]{l=2 icon=magic ability="An iconoclast gains the ability to ‹spell/detect magic› at will." replace="Detect alignment"}

::ab[Dispelling Attack (Sp)]{l=5 icon=magic standard="Once per day, an iconoclast can make a melee or ranged attack against an opponent, and if she hits, she can affect that opponent as if she had cast ‹spell/dispel magic›, using the targeted dispel option. If she misses, this ability is wasted. Use her inquisitor level as the caster level of the dispel magic effect." replace="Discern lies"}

::ab[Negating Critical (Sp)]{l=14 icon=lower ability="When an iconoclast confirms a critical hit, the creature she hit must succeed at a Fortitude save at DC 10 + 1/2 the iconoclast's caster level + the iconoclast's Wisdom modifier for each non-artifact magic item the target is wearing or carrying. On a failed saving throw, the target of the critical hit can neither use nor gain benefit from any ‹misc/minor magic item› for 1d4 rounds. This ability can be used in conjunction with critical feats." replace="Exploit weakness"}

::ab[Destroy Artifact (Sp)]{l=20 icon=magic ability="An iconoclast has the ability to destroy minor ‹rule/artifacts›. The iconoclast must have possession of the minor artifact for at least a week, which she must spend in uninterrupted contemplation of the nature of the artifact. At the end of that week, she makes a DC 30 Spellcraft check to unweave the fabric of the item, and if she is successful, the artifact is destroyed. If the iconoclast fails, she cannot try to destroy the artifact again with this ability, but another iconoclast can." replace="True judgment"}

immolator.name: Immolator
description: ## Immolator
‹SOURCE Advanced Race Guide/128›

The immolator puts her pyromaniacal urges to work in the service of a deity. She brings burning retribution down upon the enemies of her faith, consigning their souls to the sacrificial flames. An immolator has the following class features.
::prereq{r=Ifrit other="Must worship a deity whose portfolio includes the ‹domain/Fire› domain"}

::ab[Servant of the Flame]{icon=boost passive="An immolator who selects the Fire domain (or one of its associated subdomains, if available) uses her domain powers at +1 caster level (this stacks with the ifrit's fire affinity racial trait)."}
::ab[Judgment (Su)]{head icon=info note="An immolator gains the following judgment." replace="Smiting judgment"}

::ab[Immolation]{next flavor="The immolator channels purifying flame to consume her enemies." icon=lower passive="When dealing fire damage to an opponent, she treats the target's fire resistance as 5 lower than normal (minimum 0)." incrementPlain="She treats the target's resistance~1~5~10~5" incrementDesc=as incrementEnd=" lower than normal."}

::ab[Burnt Offering (Su)]{l=5 icon=boost swift="An immolator can imbue one of her weapons with the ‹magic-enh/flaming› weapon special ability. Any creature slain by this weapon burns with magical flame; its body turns to ash, though its equipment is not harmed. This special ability only functions while the immolator wields the weapon." useL=inquisitor useNC imp12="This ability gives the immolator's weapon the ‹magic-enh/flaming burst› weapon special ability." replace="Bane~greater bane"}

::ab[Judgment by Fire (Su)]{l=20 icon=boost flavor="An immolator can call fiery judgment down upon a foe during combat." swift="Whenever an immolator uses her judgment ability, she can invoke a judgment by fire on a foe. Once declared, the immolator can make a single melee (or ranged attack, if the foe is within 30 feet) against the target. If the attack hits, the attack deals fire damage instead of weapon damage, and the target must make a successful Fortitude save or die (creatures immune to fire do not have to save). The DC of this save is equal to 10 + 1/2 the immolator's level + the immolator's Wisdom modifier. Regardless of whether the save is successful, the target creature is immune to the immolator's judgment by fire ability for 24 hours." special="Creatures killed in this manner explode in a burst of fire, dealing 10d6 points of fire damage to every creature within 5 feet (Reflex save for half damage, DC 10 + 1/2 the immolator's level + the immolator's Wisdom modifier). Once this ability has been used, it cannot be used again for 1d4 rounds. This ability otherwise functions as *true judgment.*" alter="True judgment"}

infiltrator.name: Infiltrator
description: ## Infiltrator
‹SOURCE Ultimate Magic/45›
This inquisitor uses guile and deception to blend in among the enemies of the faith rather than confronting them head-on.

::ab[Misdirection (Sp)]{l=1 icon=magic ability="Each day when the infiltrator prepares spells, she may choose an alignment. She detects as that alignment as if she had used ‹spell/misdirection› on a creature with that alignment (this does not change any divination results about her other than her alignment)." alter="Stern gaze"}

::ab[Guileful Lore (Ex)]{l=1 icon=boost flavor="The infiltrator's will is bent toward subterfuge and deception." passive="She adds her Wisdom modifier on Bluff and Diplomacy skill checks in addition to the normal ability score modifiers." replace="Monster lore"}

::ab[Forbidden Lore (Ex)]{flavor="While other inquisitors learned to track unbelievers, an infiltrator learns how to cast their spells." icon=magic ability="An infiltrator can cast spells of an alignment opposed to her or her deity (ignoring the restriction in the Chaotic, Evil, Good, and Lawful Spells class ability)." replace=Track}

::ab[Necessary Lies (Su)]{icon=def passive="An infiltrator adds her class level on saving throws against abilities that detect lies or reveal or force the truth, such as ‹spell/discern lies› and ‹spell/zone of truth›." replace="Discern lies"}

keeper_of_construct.name: Keeper of Construct
description: ## Keeper of Construct
‹SOURCE People of the Wastes/27›
Some of Brigh's inquisitors, and occasionally those who follow Torag, track down malicious construct crafters and fight renegade constructs with unrivaled expertise.

::ab[Penetrating Blows (Ex)]{icon=boost flavor="A keeper of constructs' attacks are divinely guided to pierce the defenses of constructs." passive="The keeper of constructs treats her weapons as ‹eq-material/adamantine› for the purpose of overcoming damage reduction when attacking constructs." imp8="She ignores the first 5 points of DR or hardness when attacking constructs." imp16="She ignores the first 10 points of DR or hardness when attacking constructs." replace=Domain}

::ab[Construct Influence (Ex)]{icon=power flavor="A keeper of constructs' knowledge of constructs' inner workings allows her to gain an upper hand over constructs." ability="She can use the ‹skill/Intimidate› skill to demoralize constructs, and when she attempts to do so, she gains a bonus on the check equal to half her inquisitor level (minimum 1)." replace="Stern gaze"}

::ab[Construct Lore (Ex)]{icon=boost passive="A keeper of constructs adds twice her Wisdom modifier plus her Intelligence modifier as a bonus when attempting Knowledge checks to identify the abilities and weaknesses of constructs." replace="Monster lore"}

::ab[Wrest Control (Sp)]{l=14 icon=magic usage="Three times per day" standard="The keeper of constructs can issue a command to a construct. This functions as a ‹spell/suggestion› spell that is ‹misc/dismissible›, affects the construct despite its normal immunity to mind-affecting effects, and has a caster level equal to the keeper of constructs' inquisitor level. The construct can resist the command with a successful Will saving throw (DC = 10 + half the keeper of constructs' inquisitor level + her Wisdom modifier). A construct that successfully saves is immune to this ability for the following 24 hours. This effect immediately ends if the construct is attacked while under its influence." special="If an affected construct was being controlled by a creature, that individual can regain control of the construct with a successful Charisma check (attempting this check requires a standard action) opposed by the inquisitor's Charisma check. On a success, the original creature regains full control of the construct and this effect ends." replace="Exploit weakness"}

keeper_of_the_current.name: Keeper of the Current
description: ## Keeper of the Current
‹SOURCE Blood of the Sea/24›

Aquatic creatures worship many different deities and powers based on their alignment, their community, and their culture, including ‹faith/Gozreh›, ‹faith/Hei Feng›, ‹faith/Kelizandri›, and the fearsome demon lord ‹faith/Dagon›. These deities all have organized churches beneath the waves that engage zealous followers to hunt down apostates and enemies. Regardless of their faith, these undersea zealots are known as keepers of the current.

::ab[Underwater Hunter (Ex)]{icon=boost flavor="Tracking creatures underwater is particularly challenging, but a keeper of the current can read traces of eddies in the water to follow her prey." passive="A keeper of the current adds her level as a bonus on Survival checks to follow creatures underwater. She gains no bonus when finding or following tracks out of the water." replace=Track}

::ab[Marine Magic]{l=5 icon=learn ability="A keeper of the current can learn a spell with the water descriptor from the ‹main/spells_>cleric›, ‹main/spells_>druid›, inquisitor, or ‹main/wizard spell« list› in place of a spell she already knows. In effect, she loses an old spell in exchange for a new one. The new spell's level must be the same as that of the spell being exchanged, and it must be at least 1 level lower than the highest-level inquisitor spell she can cast." special="The keeper of the current can swap out only a single spell at any given level and must choose whether to swap the spell at the same time she gains new spells known for the level." incrementOrd="She can~5~3" incrementDesc="swap out a~learn a" incrementEnd=" spell, following the resrictions above." alter=Spellcasting}

::ab[Rudderless Attack (Ex)]{l=5 icon=lower passive="An opponent that takes extra damage from the keeper of the current's *bane* or *greater bane* ability has its swim speed halved for 1d4 rounds. A successful Fortitude saving throw (DC = 10 + half the inquisitor's level + her Wisdom modifier) negates this effect. If the keeper of the current targets a creature already affected by this ability, its swim speed is not further reduced but the duration of the effect is extended by 1d4 rounds." replace="Discern lies"}

kinslayer.name: Kinslayer
description: ## Kinslayer
‹SOURCE Advanced Race Guide/98›

Appalled and guilt-ridden by the horrific circumstances of her birth, a kinslayer dedicates herself to eradicating the very creatures whose blood flows within her veins. She spends her life hunting and slaying those vampiric monsters for whom humans have become prey. A kinslayer has the following class features.
::prereq{r=Dhampir}
::ab[Judgment (Su)]{head l=1 icon=power note="A kinslayer gains the following judgment." replace="*Destruction* judgment"}

::ab[Slayer's Brand (Su)]{next icon=touch-magic ability="When using this judgment, the kinslayer gains the ability to brand undead creatures with positive energy. To do so, she must make a successful melee touch attack against the undead creature. This attack deals an amount of positive energy damage equal to 1d6 + the kinslayer's Charisma modifier, and burns her personal symbol into the undead creature's flesh, bone, or even its incorporeal form. From that point onward, the kinslayer can sense the existence of the branded creature as if it were the target of a ‹spell/locate creature› spell (caster level equal to 1/2 the kinslayer's inquisitor level)." special="A *slayer's brand* lasts until the undead creature is destroyed or until the kinslayer uses this ability on another creature."}

::ab[Greater Brand (Su)]{head l=1 icon=power flavor="A kinslayer learns to modify her *slayer's brand* judgment as she gains levels." ability="Whenever she gains the ability to learn a teamwork feat, she can instead opt to learn one of the following modifications to her *slayer's brand* judgment." special="Each time the kinslayer opts to take a greater brand, it replaces her ability to gain a teamwork feat at that level." alter="Teamwork feats"}

::ab[Branding Ray]{next icon=zap ability="The *slayer's brand* judgment can be used as a ranged touch attack with a range of 20 feet." special="A kinslayer can take this modification more than once. Whenever she does, she increases the range of her *brand* by 20 feet."}
::ab[Debilitating Brand]{next icon=def passive="A creature currently affected by the *slayer's brand* judgment takes a -2 penalty on attack rolls against the kinslayer who placed the *brand.*"}

::ab[Devastating Brand]{next icon=lower-boost ability="When the kinslayer attacks a creature that she has branded with her *slayer's brand,* she threatens a critical hit on a roll of 19-20. If the kinslayer is good, she also gains a +2 sacred bonus on all rolls to confirm critical hits on a branded creature."}
::ab[Dual Brand]{next icon=boost ability="The kinslayer can have up to two creatures branded at a time."}
::ab[Holy Brand]{next icon=boost ability="The kinslayer can use her *brand* on creatures with the evil subtype as well as undead."}

::ab[Searing Brand]{next icon=boost prereq="6th-Level Inquisitor" ability="The kinslayer's *slayer's brand* deals an additional 1d6 points of damage for every five inquisitor levels she has. If the creature hit with the *slayer's brand* is particularly vulnerable to bright light, the damage dice of her *slayer's brand* increases to d8s."}
::ab[Silver Brand]{next icon=boost ability="The kinslayer can use her *brand* on lycanthropes and creatures with vulnerability to silver as well as undead."}

::ab[Undead Sense (Sp)]{l=2 icon=magic-boost ability="A kinslayer gains the ability to use ‹spell/detect undead› as a spell-like ability (caster level equal to the kinslayer's inquisitor level) at will. If she detects the presence of undead, she can use her *monster lore* ability to attempt to determine the type of undead detected as well as to reveal any strengths or weaknesses the undead might have. If any of the detected undead are vampires, she gains a bonus on the check equal to her inquisitor level to immediately identify them as such." replace="Detect alignment"}

living_grimoire.name: Living Grimoire
description: ## Living Grimoire
‹SOURCE Horror Adventures/53›

The living grimoire literally wields the sacred word of his deity, using his holy tome to smite the foes of his god with divine might. Unlike most inquisitors, a living grimoire focuses on careful study of divine scripture, valuing knowledge over intuition.

::ab[Holy Book (Su)]{l=1 icon=melee-magic flavor="A living grimoire forms a supernatural bond with a large ironbound tome containing the holy text of his deity and learns to use it as a weapon." ability="When wielding the *holy book* as a weapon, he deals base damage as if it were a ‹eq-material/cold iron› ‹eq-weapon/light mace› (but see *Sacred Word* below), is considered proficient with the book, takes no ‹misc/improvised weapon› penalty, and gains a +1 bonus on attack rolls with the book. The tome serves as his holy symbol and divine focus, and can be enchanted as a magic weapon. He can replace his bonded tome with another book at any time, though he must perform a 24-hour binding ritual to attune himself to the new book." replace="Monster lore"}
:::ab{title=Spells icon=magic action=Ability alter=Orisons~spellcasting replace="Cunning initiative"}

A living grimoire must prepare his spells ahead of time, and can know any number of inquisitor spells. He uses Intelligence instead of Wisdom as his key spellcasting ability score (to determine his spell DCs, ‹rule/bonus spells› per day, modifier on concentration checks, and so on), and to determine the effects and number of uses of his domain powers.

The living grimoire receives the same number of spell slots per day as a ‹class/warpriest› of his inquisitor level, and receives bonus spells per day if he has a high Intelligence score. He must choose and prepare his spells ahead of time by choosing a time each day for 1 hour of quiet contemplation while he studies his *holy book.*

At 1st level, the living grimoire's *holy book* contains all 0-level inquisitor spells plus three 1st-level inquisitor spells of his choice. He also selects a number of additional 1st-level inquisitor spells equal to his Intelligence modifier to add to his *holy book.* At each new inquisitor level, he gains two new inquisitor spells of any spell levels he can cast (based on his new inquisitor level) for his *holy book.* At any time, he can learn inquisitor spells from scrolls or other written sources and add inquisitor spells he finds to his *holy book.*

::ab[Sacred Word (Su)]{l=1 icon=boost ability="A living grimoire learns to charge his *holy book* with the power of his faith. The inquisitor gains the benefits of the warpriest's *sacred weapon* class ability, but the benefits apply only to his bonded *holy book.* Like a warpriest's *sacred weapon,* the living grimoire's *book* deals damage based on the inquisitor's level, not the *book's* base damage (unless the inquisitor chooses to use the *book's* base damage)." imp4="The living grimoire gains the ability to enhance his *holy book* with divine power as a @HLswift_action. This ability grants the *holy book* an enhancement bonus equal to ‹misc/one-fourth› of her inquisitor level. This bonus stack with any existing bonuses the *holy book* might have, to a maximum of +5. The living grimoire can enhance his *holy book* to have any of the special abilities listed in the warpriest's *sacred weapon* ability, subject to the same alignment restrictions, but adds ‹magic-enh/bane› to the general special ability list. Adding any of these special abilities to the *holy book* consumes an amount of enhancement bonus equal to the special ability's base price modifier. The *holy book* must have at least a +1 enhancement bonus before the living grimoire can add any special abilities to it." useL=inquisitor useNC special="As with the warpriest *sacred weapon* ability, he determines the enhancement bonus and special abilities the first time he uses the ability each day, and they cannot be changed until the next day." replace=Judgment}

::ab[Blessed Script (Su)]{l=5 icon=magic ability="A living grimoire can permanently tattoo one spell of 2nd level or lower from his *holy book* onto his body. The tattooed spell cannot have an expensive material component or focus. The living grimoire can prepare any tattooed spell without needing his *holy book,* and can cast his tattooed spells as a spell-like ability once per day." imp8="The living grimoire can tattoo one additional spell of 3rd level or lower upon himself." imp12="He can tattoo one additional spell of 4th level or lower upon himself." imp16="He can tattoo a final additional spell of any level upon himself." special="When the living grimoire uses these spell-like abilities, the tattooed holy script associated with the spell-like ability glows as per ‹spell/light› for a number of rounds equal to the spell's level." replace="Bane~greater bane~second judgment~third judgment"}

::ab[Word of God (Su)]{l=20 icon=melee flavor="A living grimoire can smite his foes with the holy word of his deity." usage="Up to seven times per day." ability="The inquisitor can make a single melee attack with his *holy book* against a target. If the attack hits, it deals damage normally and the target must succeed at a Fortitude save or die (DC = 10 + 1/2 the living grimoire's inquisitor level + his Intelligence modifier). Regardless of whether the save is successful, the target creature is immune to the living grimoire's word of god ability for 24 hours." special="Once the living grimoire uses this ability, he can't use it again for 1d4 rounds." replace="True judgment"}

monster_tactician.name: Monster Tactician
description: ## Monster Tactician
‹SOURCE Monster Summoner's Handbook/7›

While most inquisitors have learned to take advantage of the movements of their opponents and allies in combat, some instead summon creatures as a means of claiming strategic advantage, exploiting the mystic connection between themselves and their summoned creatures to impart a bit of their own tactical knowledge.

::ab[Summon Monster (Sp)]{icon=magic useMod=Wisdom3 l1="A monster tactician can cast ‹spell/summon monster I› as a spell-like ability. She can cast this spell as a @HLstandard_action, and the creatures remain for 1 minute per level (instead of 1 round per level). **At 3rd level** and **every 2** inquisitor levels thereafter, the power of this ability increases by 1 spell level, allowing her to summon more powerful creatures (to a maximum of ‹spell/summon monster IX› at 17th level)." special="A monster tactician cannot have more than one *summon monster* spell active in this way at a time; if she uses another, any existing *summon monster* immediately ends. These *summon monster* spells are considered to be part of the monster tactician's spell list for the purposes of spell trigger and spell completion items. In addition, she can expend uses of this ability to fulfill the construction requirements of any magic items she creates, so long as she can use this ability to cast the required spells" replace=Judgment imp2="Beginning at **2nd level** and **each level** thereafter, the monster tactician can add one monster from the ‹feat/Expanded Summon Monster› list to her list of available summoned creatures. She can add a new option to any *summon monster* spell she can cast at the time she chooses the monster; once made, these choices cannot be changed."}

::ab[Summon Tactics (Su)]{l=5 icon=power ability="Every time a monster tactician casts a summoning spell, she can choose any one teamwork feat she knows. All creatures summoned by the spell are treated as if they had the chosen feat for all purposes, without needing to meet any prerequisites." imp8="She can choose two different teamwork feats." imp16="She can choose three teamwork feats. She cannot choose the same feat twice." imp20="All creatures summoned by the spell are treated as if they knew all of the monster tactician's teamwork feats." replace="Discern lies~second judgment~third judgment~slayer~true judgment"}

oathkeeper.name: Oathkeeper
description: ## Oathkeeper
‹SOURCE Merchant's Manifest/30›

Servants of gods who hold oaths to be sacrosanct, oathkeepers formally oversee the creation of bargains and personally hunt down those who dare to violate them. Many of these inquisitors previously oversaw religious vows in more formal settings, though more pursue more lucrative contracts. Wronged merchants or cheated customers often seek out oathkeepers to bring justice when local authorities fail.
::ab[Alignment]{icon=warning note="An oathkeeper must be of a lawful alignment, and her alignment must be within one step of her deity's." alter="Alignment requirements"}
::cskill{gain=app lose=kna}
:::ab{title="Divine Witness (Su)" replace="Monster lore" icon=power action=Ability}

Every oathkeeper inquisitor develops a unique personal symbol, which she can use to seal an agreement between two or more willing parties. The agreement, whether it's a trade contract, a truce, or a political marriage, must be as detailed as possible and then signed in the oathkeeper's presence by every creature involved. Creatures must sign of their own free will - any magical or physical coercion will cause the contract to crumble into ash when the oathkeeper attempts to place her seal upon it. So long as no mystic or physical force is influencing any participating creature, the oathkeeper can mystically seal the arrangement with her personal symbol, binding all signing parties into upholding their ends of the bargain for the length of time spelled out in the agreement.

Should any oathbound creature break the exact wording of the contract, the agreement is dissolved and the offending creature is cursed, taking a penalty on all d20 rolls equal to the oathkeeper's Wisdom modifier for a number of weeks equal to the oathkeeper's inquisitor level. Furthermore, the inquisitor's symbol appears as a mystic symbol on the offending creature's throat and hands, allowing the oathkeeper to know the creature's location at all times, as per the ‹spell/discern location› spell.

If a creature is forced into breaking the agreement by magic or overwhelming physical force (for example, breaking an agreement not to harm via a ‹spelldef/compulsion› spell or by physically hurling an oathbound creature to deal damage to another), then the agreement dissipates harmlessly. The oathkeeper's curse is a curse effect, but it can be removed only with a ‹spell/miracle› or ‹spell/wish› spell.

::ab[Oathbreaker's Scourge (Su)]{l=2 icon=boost-warning ability="If an oathkeeper is given physical evidence of a broken agreement and asked to hunt down the contract's breaker by the offended party, the oathkeeper gains a +4 sacred or profane bonus on Diplomacy and Survival skill checks to gather information about and track the target. The inquisitor is also treated as being 3 levels higher for the purpose of one judgment she uses against the deal breaker, so long as she keeps possession of the evidence." special="If the oathkeeper uses oathbreaker's scourge when attacking someone who is actually innocent of fraud - due to forgery, hasty judgments, or other deceptions - she loses access to all of her oathkeeper archetype abilities for 1 month or until she is the target of an ‹spell/atonement› spell." replace=Track}

preacher.name: Preacher
description: ## Preacher
‹SOURCE Ultimate Magic/46›

Some inquisitors wander the land to spread the true word of their faith. Often they come into conflict with those hostile to their teachings or to the preacher's need to help those who cannot help themselves. The leaders of evil or aggressive religions send these preachers into new territories to win converts and hopefully allies. Often, they start uprisings against powers hostile to their religion, or defend a group of honest believers from the depredations of the unfaithful.

::ab[Determination (Ex)]{head l=3 icon=power flavor="The preacher is a person of few words on the battlefield, but those words hold great power and authority." ability="Once per day, the inquisitor can use this ability to create one of the effects below." replace="Solo tactics" alter="Teamwork feats" repeat="Instead of selecting a bonus teamwork feat, you may increase the number of uses per day of this ability by one.~6~3"}

::ab[Aggression]{next icon=roll free="The preacher may reroll an attack roll that she just made before the results of the roll are revealed. She must take the result of the reroll, even if it's worse than the original roll."}

::ab[Defense]{next icon=def immediate="When the inquisitor would be hit by a melee or ranged attack, she may add a +4 insight bonus to her Armor Class against that attack, and if this makes the inquisitor's AC higher than the opponent's attack roll, the attack misses."}

::ab[Warning]{next icon=protect-roll free="When a preacher's ally within line of sight would be hit by a melee or ranged attack, she may call out a warning to that ally, and the attacker must reroll the attack and use the results of the second roll. The ally must be able to hear the preacher and must not be ‹misc/helpless› for this ability to have any effect."}

ravener_hunter.name: Ravener Hunter
description: ## Ravener Hunter
‹SOURCE Blood of the Beast/4›

Throughout the Mwangi Expanse, cults of ‹faith/Angazhan› pollute the pristine jungle with demonic influence and wanton bloodshed. For generations, the ‹race/catfolk› of Murraseth have viewed such faiths with loathing and hatred, and they believe it is their sacred duty to hunt down the followers of the Ravener King and expel them from the Material Plane.
::prereq{r=Catfolk other="Alignment must be nonevil"}

::ab[Charged by Nature]{flavor="Rather than having a deity patron, a ravener hunter is charged by the spirits of the Mwangi to eradicate evil wherever it appears." icon=power choice="A ravener hunter chooses an oracle ‹ability/mystery› from the following list: ‹mystery/ancestor›, ‹mystery/battle›, ‹mystery/flame›, ‹mystery/heavens›, ‹mystery/life›, ‹mystery/lunar›, ‹mystery/nature›, ‹mystery/solar›, ‹mystery/stone›, ‹mystery/time›, ‹mystery/volcano›, ‹mystery/waves›, ‹mystery/wind›, or ‹mystery/wood›." ability="She gains one revelation from her chosen mystery. She must meet the revelation's prerequisites, using her inquisitor level as her effective ‹class/oracle› level to determine the revelation's effects, and she never qualifies for the ‹feat/Extra Revelation› feat." imp8="The ravener hunter gains a second revelation from her chosen mystery." order=ca replace=Domain}

::ab[Holy Magic]{icon=learn note="A ravener hunter adds all spells of 6th-level and lower on the ‹main/cleric spell« list› with the good descriptor to her inquisitor spell list as inquisitor spells of the same level. If a spell appears on both the cleric and inquisitor spell lists, the ravener hunter uses the lower of the two spell levels listed for the spell. She cannot cast spells@FN1 with the chaotic, evil, or lawful descriptors, even from spell trigger or spell completion items." alter=Spells}

::ab[Demon Hunter]{l=3 icon=power-boost ability="A ravener hunter gains ‹feat/Demon Hunter› as a bonus feat, ignoring its prerequisites." order=ap passive="She also gains a +2 bonus on Knowledge (religion) checks to recognize the worshipers of any deity with the Demon subdomain, as well as a +2 morale bonus on attack rolls and caster level checks to overcome spell resistance of creatures that she recognizes as followers of such a deity." replace="Teamwork feat gained at 3rd level"}
::ab[Solo Tactics (Ex)]{l=6 icon=info note="A ravener hunter gains this ability at 6th level instead of 3rd level."}
:::fakeFootnotes
@FN[Edited; the original text reads: "She cannot cast **2** with the chaotic..."]{from=1}

reaper_of_secrets.name: Reaper of Secrets
description: ## Reaper of Secrets
‹SOURCE Dirty Tactics Toolbox/17›
Masters of mind tricks and deception, these divine assassins track down and kill anyone who has forbidden knowledge about their faiths.

::ab[Bound by Secrecy (Ex)]{icon=warning note="A reaper of secrets must choose ‹faith/Norgorber› as her deity. If she ever changes her deity, she loses this archetype and becomes a normal inquisitor."}
::ab[Deceitful Lore (Ex)]{l=1 icon=boost passive="A reaper of secrets adds her Wisdom modifier on Bluff and Disguise skill checks in addition to her Charisma modifier." replace="Monster lore"}

::ab[Soul-Piercing Gaze (Su)]{l=1 icon=boost swift="A reaper of secrets can focus on a single creature she can see. She then gains an insight bonus on Perception and Sense Motive checks against that creature equal to 1/2 her level (minimum +1). The effect ends if she focuses on a new target." replace="Stern gaze"}

::ab[Mind-Game Tactics (Su)]{l=3 icon=boost flavor="A reaper of secrets can read other creatures so well she can use their reactions to gain a tactical advantage." ability="She treats any creature targeted by her *soul-piercing gaze* as if it were her ally and possessed the same teamwork feats as she does for the purposes of positioning and threatening when she determines whether she receives a benefit from her teamwork feats. She cannot use this ability to benefit from any teamwork feats that require particular actions from allies, such as ‹feat/Swap Places›." special="For example, a reaper of secrets with the ‹feat/Precise Strike› feat gains the bonus from that feat if she and the target of her *soul-piercing gaze* occupy flanking positions relative to the creature she attacks." replace="Solo tactics"}

relic_hunter.name: Relic Hunter
description: ## Relic Hunter
‹SOURCE Occult Adventures/119›

Some inquisitors specialize in the use and recovery of long-lost relics of their faiths, drawing forth divine might from the recovered items in order to restore their sanctity and wield these artifacts against the enemies of their gods.

::ab[Spells]{icon=learn-magic note="A relic hunter casts divine spells drawn from the inquisitor spell list, but her chosen relic schools (see *Relics* below) limit her to schools she knows, similar in function to an occultist's *implements.* Any spell or orison gained by the relic hunter has a relic component (see *Relics* below), and is not considered to be on the relic hunter's spell list until she selects its school though the relics alternate class feature. Once she chooses a school, all spells and orisons of that school are considered to be on the relic hunter's class spell list, allowing her to use spell trigger and spell completion magic items of those spells." special="A relic hunter's spells per day, daily spell allotment, Difficulty Class, and minimum Wisdom score requirements remain unchanged from the inquisitor's normal spellcasting class feature." alter=Spellcasting}

::ab[Relics (Su)]{l=1 icon=magic ability="A relic hunter gains the ‹class/occultist«'s› *implements* class feature and learns to use two occultist ‹ability/implement« school»s› as relic schools." imp4="The relic hunter learns to use one additional relic school drawn from the same source, gaining access to that school's resonant power and base focus power and opening up that school's focus powers for her to select. Like an occultist, a relic hunter can select the same school twice, but it is far less useful for her to do so." repeat="She can learn a new relic school.~7~3" special="Relics do not need to be magic items, and nonmagical relics do not take up a magic item slot. Relics that are not magic items are often of some religious significance to the relic hunter or her church, such as the battered shield of a saint, a bishop's robe, or the finger bone of a church martyr." replace=Judgment}

:::ab{l=1 icon=magic title="Deific Focus (Su)" action=Ability replace="Domain~bane~greater bane~second judgment~third judgment" repeat="She learns a new focus power selected from all of the powers granted by all of the relic schools she knows.~4~4" special="Unless otherwise noted, the DC for any saving throws against a focus power is equal to 10 + 1/2 the inquisitor's class level + the inquisitor's Wisdom modifier. She cannot select a focus power more than once. She uses her inquisitor level in place of an occultist level to qualify for focus powers."}
A relic hunter learns to invest divine power into her chosen relics. This acts like the occultist's *focus powers* and *mental focus* class features, with the following adjustments.

Each day, a relic hunter has a number of points of *deific focus* equal to her inquisitor level + her Wisdom modifier, and she must spend 1 hour in prayer with her relics to invest them with divine power. These points refresh at the start of each day.

The relic hunter learns the two base focus powers from her chosen relic schools and can select one more focus power from the list of those made available by her chosen schools. Whenever she gains a new relic school, she gains the base focus power of that school. She can use these focus powers only by expending points of *deific focus.*

royal_accuser.name: Royal Accuser
description: ## Royal Accuser
‹SOURCE Inner Sea Intrigue/45›
Royal accusers serve the prince of Ustalav, cutting through the nation's baroque politics and quietly facing its many threats.
::cskill{gain=kl lose=kna}
::ab[Meticulous Inspection (Ex)]{icon=boost passive="A royal accuser gains a morale bonus equal to 1/2 his inquisitor level on all Perception checks (minimum +1)." replace="Stern gaze"}

::ab[Detect Monsters and Secrets (Sp)]{l=2 icon=magic ability="A royal accuser can use ‹spell/detect aberration›, ‹spell/detect secret doors›, and ‹spell/detect undead› at will. He can only use one of these at any given time." replace="Detect alignment"}

::ab[Favored Enemy (Ex)]{l=3 icon=power ability="A royal accuser gains ‹ability/favored enemy›, as the ‹class/ranger› class feature. He must choose his favored enemy from the following list: aberration, dragon, humanoid (orc), humanoid (shapechanger), and undead." incrementOrd="He gains~3~6" incrementDesc=a incrementEnd=" favored enemy." replace="Solo tactics~teamwork feats gained at 3rd, 9th, and 15th levels"}
:::ab{l=6 title="Informed Hunch (Su)" icon=magic action=Ability replace="Teamwork feat granted at 6th level"}

Once per day, a royal accuser can contemplate an ongoing investigation he is involved in and potentially know whether his hunches are on the right track. This functions as ‹spell/augury›, except if it succeeds, the royal accuser gets one of the following results in place of weal, woe, or weal and woe. The royal accuser treats his inquisitor level as his caster level.
- **Correct** (if the hunch is the exact truth, such as if a particular individual is responsible for a crime, or if a particular organization is behind a series of events).
- **Incorrect** (if the hunch is entirely off base, such as if a suspected individual had nothing to do with a crime, or a suspected organization had no interest or hand in a series of events).

- **On the right track** (if the hunch is partially correct, such as if a suspected individual participated in a criminal conspiracy or had foreknowledge of the crime in question, or if a suspected organization directly profited from and knew about but did not entirely orchestrate a series of events).
::ab[Greater Detect Magic (Sp)]{l=12 icon=magic ability="A royal accuser can use ‹spell/greater detect magic› at will." replace="Teamwork feat granted at 12th"}

::ab[Final Sway (Ex)]{l=18 icon=boost flavor="A royal accuser's word and intuition are greatly trusted and valued." ability="Once per day, when using the ‹rule/influence« system› and interacting with an individual or organization with whom he has at least minor sway or is respected (rank 2), the royal accuser can treat his influence as two steps higher for the purposes of making a single request of that individual or organization. Alternatively, he can treat an individual's attitude toward him as up to two steps higher for the purpose of such a request." replace="Teamwork feat granted at 18th level"}

sacred_huntsmaster.name: Sacred Huntsmaster
description: ## Sacred Huntsmaster
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/99›

Some inquisitors create a strong bond with an animal companion, and they hunt and punish threats to the faith as an awe-inspiring duo. When they work together as one, there are few that dare to stand in their way.

::ab[Animal Companion (Ex)]{l=1 icon=power ability="A sacred huntsmaster forms a bond with an ‹sidekick/animal companion›. This ability works as the ‹class/hunter› class feature of the same name, using her inquisitor level as her hunter level." replace="Judgment 1/day"}

::ab[Hunter Tactics (Ex)]{l=3 icon=power passive="A sacred huntsmaster automatically grants her teamwork feats to her animal companion. The companion doesn't need to meet the prerequisites of these teamwork feats." replace="Solo tactics"}

::ab[Animal Focus (Su)]{l=4 icon=power swift="A sacred huntsmaster can take on the aspects of an animal. This ability works as the hunter's ‹ability/animal focus› class feature, with her inquisitor level serving as her hunter level." replace="Later iterations of the judgment ability"}

::ab[Improved Empathic Link (Su)]{l=8 icon=power ability="The sacred huntsmaster gains an empathic link with her animal companion. This functions like an empathic link with a ‹sidekick/familiar›, except the sacred huntsmaster can also see through a companion's eyes as a @HLswift_action, maintaining this connection as long as she likes (as long as the companion is within 1 mile) and ending it as a @HLfree_action." special="The sacred huntsmaster is ‹misc/blinded› while maintaining this connection." replace="Second judgment"}

::ab[Raise Animal Companion (Sp)]{l=16 icon=magic ability="A sacred huntsmaster gains ‹spell/raise animal companion› as a spell-like ability (not restricted to her own animal companion). Using this ability gives the sacred huntsmaster a ‹rule/negative level› that cannot be overcome in any way (including by ‹spell/restoration›), but automatically ends after 24 hours." imp16="This functions as ‹spell/resurrection› instead of ‹spell/raise dead›, but otherwise operates as normal." replace="Third judgment"}

::ab[Second Animal Focus (Ex)]{l=17 icon=boost ability="Whenever a sacred huntsmaster uses her *animal focus* ability, she selects two different animal aspects for herself instead of one, and can assign two aspects to her companion instead of one. As with the companion's previous aspect, the second one does not count against the minutes per day a sacred huntsmaster can take on an aspect. If her animal companion is dead and the sacred huntsmaster has applied that aspect to herself, that aspect counts toward her maximum of two aspects at once. The sacred huntsmaster can still only apply one of her dead companion's to herself, not both." replace=Slayer}

::ab[Greater Empathic Link (Su)]{l=20 icon=boost-power ability="The range of a sacred huntsmaster's empathic link with her animal companion increases to 10 miles. If the animal companion is within 1 mile, it and the sacred huntsmaster can communicate telepathically." replace="True judgment"}

sanctified_slayer.name: Sanctified Slayer
description: ## Sanctified Slayer
‹SOURCE Advanced Class Guide/99›

While all inquisitors root out enemies of the faith, in many orders and churches there's a select group of these religious hunters devoted to one goal, and one goal alone - to terminate the enemies of the faith wherever they can be found. Sometimes these sanctified slayers are given special dispensation to commit ruthless murders for the faith's greater good. Other times, they're simply willing to take the initiative to revel in the zeal of such grisly work.

::ab[Studied Target (Ex)]{l=1 icon=power ability="A sanctified slayer gains the ‹class/slayer«'s› *studied target* class feature. She uses her inquisitor level as her effective slayer level to determine the effects of *studied target.*" replace="Judgment 1/day" head}

::ab[Studied Target (Ex)]{flavor="Text from the slayer class" icon=boost next move="You can study an opponent you can see. You then gain a bonus on Bluff, Knowledge, Perception, Sense Motive, and Survival checks attempted against that opponent, and a bonus on weapon attack and damage rolls against it; these bonuses are equal to 1 + ‹misc/one-fifth› of your slayer level. The DCs of slayer class abilities against that opponent increase by 1 + one-fifth of your slayer level. You can only maintain these bonuses against a certain number of opponents at a time (equal to 1 + one-fifth of your slayer level); these bonuses remain in effect until either the opponent is dead or you study a new target." order=mif immediate="If you deal sneak attack damage to a target, you can study that target immediately, allowing you to apply your studied target bonuses against that target (including to the normal weapon damage roll)." imp7="You can now choose to study a target as a @HLswift_action." free="You can discard this connection to a studied target, allowing you to study another target in its place."}
:::ab{l=4 icon=power replace="Later iterations of the *judgment* ability" title="Sneak Attack (Ex)" action=Ability incrementPlain="The additional damage~4~3" incrementEnd="d6."}

If a sanctified slayer catches an opponent unable to defend itself effectively from her attack, she can strike a vital spot to deal extra damage. The sanctified slayer's attack deals 1d6 additional damage anytime her target would be denied a Dexterity bonus to AC (whether or not the target actually has a Dexterity bonus), or when the sanctified slayer ‹rule/flanks› her target. Should a sanctified slayer score a critical hit with the sneak attack, this extra damage is not multiplied. Ranged attacks can count as sneak attacks only if the target is within 30 feet.

With a weapon that deals nonlethal damage (such as a sap, whip, or an unarmed strike), a sanctified slayer can make a sneak attack that deals nonlethal damage instead of lethal damage. She cannot use a weapon that deals lethal damage to deal nonlethal damage in a sneak attack, even with the usual -4 penalty.

A sanctified slayer must be able to see the target well enough to pick out a vital spot and must be able to reach such a spot. A sanctified slayer cannot use sneak attack while striking a creature with ‹rule/concealment›.

::ab[Talented Slayer (Ex)]{icon=power l8="A sanctified slayer can gain a single ‹ability/slayer talent<s›, including those from the list of rogue talents that a slayer can take, but not an advanced slayer talent." repeatAt="She can gain another slayer talent, as above.~16~17~20" replace="Second judgment~third judgment~slayer~true judgment"}

secret_seeker.name: Secret Seeker
description: ## Secret Seeker
‹SOURCE Ultimate Intrigue/29›

Secret seekers are trained in the art of extracting answers from intransigent foes. Recognizing that torture is a poor method of securing truthful intelligence, secret seekers instead prefer magical means of inquiry.

::ab[Occult Lore (Su)]{icon=power ability="A secret seeker gains access to the ‹skill/prognostication› and ‹skill/read aura› occult skill unlocks, but not the other occult skill unlocks." special="If she ever chooses to gain the ‹feat/Psychic Sensitivity› feat or otherwise gains access to all occult skill unlocks, she receives ‹feat/Psychic Maestro› as a bonus feat, with prognostication and read aura as the two occult skill unlocks selected." replace="Monster lore"}

::ab[Detect Mind (Sp)]{l=5 icon=magic ability="A secret seeker can ‹spell/detect anxieties›, ‹spell/detect desires›, or ‹spell/detect thoughts›, as per the spells, for a number of rounds per day equal to twice her inquisitor level. These rounds need not be consecutive. A creature that succeeds at a saving throw against this ability is immune to that secret seeker's detect mind ability for 24 hours. If the secret seeker has the ‹inquisition/secrets› inquisition, she doubles the number of rounds per day that she can use this ability." imp12="A secret seeker can use her *detect mind* ability for a number of minutes per day equal to twice her inquisitor level, rather than rounds. These minutes need not be consecutive but must be spent in 1-minute increments. If the secret seeker has the *secrets* inquisition, she doubles the number of minutes per day she can use this ability." replace="Bane~greater bane"}

::ab[Seek Mind (Sp)]{l=14 icon=magic ability="A secret seeker can use her *detect mind* ability to seek out people with particular anxieties, desires, or thoughts. This functions as per ‹spell/seek thoughts› except that it works with any of the three types of mental output the secret seeker can detect with detect mind." replace="Exploit weakness"}

sin_eater.name: Sin Eater
description: ## Sin Eater
‹SOURCE Ultimate Magic/46›

There is a sect of inquisitors in some religions that believes it is not enough to hunt the enemies of the church - one must also devour those enemies' sins. More benign versions of the practice believe that sin, or evil, is taken out of the world when a sin is devoured, denying the enemy's soul to the enemy's god and purifying the world of its taint. Followers of malevolent churches believe that consuming the sins of good folk not only corrupts the enemy soul to keep it from the celestial planes, but also taints the souls of those who witness the sin-eating or the corpse of its victim. Consuming sins empowers the sin eater, at least for a time.

::ab[Eat Sin (Sp)]{l=1 replace=Domain icon=power-aid order=frn free="When the sin eater inquisitor kills an enemy, she may eat the sins of that enemy by spending 1 minute adjacent to its corpse. This provokes attacks of opportunity." fullround="The inquisitor can rush this ritual, performing it as a full-round action that provokes attacks of opportunity, but she only gains half the normal benefit (see below)." note="Eating the enemy's sins heals the inquisitor of a number of hit points of damage equal to 1d8 + her inquisitor level (maximum +5). The enemy must have been killed by the sin eater within the last hour, and it must have had at least as many Hit Dice as half the inquisitor's level. The inquisitor can use this ability once for each enemy she kills. This ability has no effect on mindless creatures or those with Intelligence 2 or less." imp5="The healing increases to 2d8 plus her inquisitor level (maximum +10)." imp9="The healing increases to 3d8 + her inquisitor level (maximum +15)." imp13="It increases to 4d8 + her inquisitor level (maximum +20)." special="In some faiths, this &quot&eating&quot& is a purely symbolic act, while in others, the inquisitor must eat a small amount of food and water as part of the ritual. A few extreme faiths actually require the inquisitor to eat some of the body of the slain enemy." imp8="When a sin eater eats the sins of a creature that would rise as an undead (such as someone slain by a ‹monster/shadow›, ‹monster/spectre›, or ‹template/vampire›), the sin eater may choose to accept 1 temporary ‹rule/negative level› to absorb the taint in the corpse, preventing it from rising as an undead. This negative level can be removed with the appropriate magic, though it automatically expires after 24 hours, and never becomes a permanent negative level. At the GM's discretion, this ability may prevent a ‹template/ghost› from using its rejuvenation ability."}

::ab[Speak with Dead (Sp)]{l=6 icon=magic ability="When the inquisitor eats an enemy's sins, within 10 minutes of doing so, she can ask the remnants of the enemy's soul questions as if using ‹spell/speak with dead›, with a caster level equal to her inquisitor level. She does not need the enemy's corpse to use this ability (she can eat sin, move away from the corpse, then use *speak with dead*), though the soul gets a saving throw just as a corpse would." replace="Teamwork feat gained at 6th level"}

::ab[Burden of Sin (Sp)]{l=14 icon=protect fullround="A sin eater may transfer one harmful affliction, condition, or spell effect from another creature to herself (this includes curses, possessions, and permanent effects such as petrification, or any condition that ‹spell/break enchantment› can end or reverse). The effect to be transferred is chosen by the sin eater and affects her as if she were the original target, continuing its duration (if any) and preventing any further effect on the original bearer. For example, the inquisitor could transfer a lethal disease to herself, or petrify herself to restore a petrified comrade. The sin eater can use this ability as often as desired, even using it multiple times on the same creature." replace="Exploit weakness"}

spellbreaker.name: Spellbreaker
description: ## Spellbreaker
‹SOURCE Ultimate Combat/52›

The world is full of dangerous magic, and many recoil in the face of such power. The spellbreaker, by contrast, learns to recognize and resist certain types of magic, wading through waves of magic to reach her foes.

::ab[Strong-Willed (Ex)]{l=1 icon=def-roll flavor="A spellbreaker is able to stand strong against magical effects that seek to control, compel, or persuade her." passive="The spellbreaker rolls twice and takes the best result when making a Will saving throw against a mind-affecting effect." replace="Monster lore"}

::ab[Defense against Magic (Ex)]{l=3 icon=def passive="A spellbreaker picks a single ‹ability/arcane>wizard» school<s›. She gains a +1 bonus on saving throws against arcane spells of that school." incrementOrd="The spellbreaker picks~3~4" incrementDesc=a incrementEnd=" school and gains this bonus against arcane spells of that school." special="Each time the spellbreaker picks a new school, the bonuses for the schools she has already chosen increase by 1." replace="Teamwork feats"}

::ab[Foil Casting (Su)]{l=3 icon=lower passive="When an opponent tries to cast an arcane spell within a spellbreaker's threatened area, the DC for that caster to cast defensively increases by 2. This increase stacks with the effects of the ‹feat/Disruptive› feat." ability="Furthermore, the spellbreaker knows where to hit foes to foil casting from a distance. Each time she hits an arcane spellcaster or a creature that uses spell-like abilities with a ranged weapon attack, the DC of any ‹rule/concentration› checks the caster makes increases by 2 for 1 round." order=pa replace="Solo tactics"}

::ab[Impervious (Ex)]{l=20 icon=def passive="A spellbreaker becomes immune to the effects of a single school of arcane magic. That school of magic must be the first one she picked for *defense against magic* (see above). Neither harmful nor helpful arcane spells of that school have an effect on the spellbreaker. If a spell of that school is an area of effect spell, the spell goes off as normal, but the spellbreaker is untouched by its effects." swift="Once per day, the spellbreaker can grant this imperviousness to all allies in a 60-foot ‹misc/burst› for 1 minute." order=pw replace="True judgment"}

suit_seeker.name: Suit Seeker
description: ## Suit Seeker
‹SOURCE The Harrow Handbook/22›

Though surrounded by meaning and tradition revered by most harrowers, the harrow is a tool that could be abused.
